name: orders_2024_messy.csv
dataset_description: This file appears to contain a list of orders, likely from an e-commerce platform or retail business. It includes information about each order's ID, customer ID, total cost, date of the order, and status.

column_overview.name: CustID
column_overview.inferred_role: Identifier for the customer who made the order

column_overview.name: OrderDate
column_overview.inferred_role: Date when the order was placed
column_overview.notes: There appears to be a mix of date formats (YYYY-MM-DD and MM/DD/YYYY). This may cause issues with sorting or filtering by date.

column_overview.name: Status
column_overview.notes: The 'Status' column contains inconsistent capitalization, which may cause issues with case-sensitive operations (e.g., sorting or filtering).

summary: This dataset appears to be relatively clean, but there are some potential issues with date formatting and inconsistent capitalization in the 'OrderDate' and 'Status' columns.
key_problems: Inconsistent date formats in the 'OrderDate' column
recommendations: Standardize the date format to a single format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD) for consistency and ease of use.
Normalize the capitalization in the 'Status' column to a standard case (e.g., lowercase or title case)
confidence_notes: The sample size is relatively small, so it's possible that these issues may not be representative of the entire dataset.
